title: Mars has a thin atmosphere
body_text: Mars's atmosphere is about 1% as dense as Earth's and is mostly carbon dioxide (~95%). This is why liquid water cannot remain stable on the surface today.

title: Look for layered rocks
body_text: Layered (sedimentary) rocks inside craters often record past water activity. Try sampling the lowest visible layer first — it is typically the oldest.

title: Why Mars's seasons are uneven
body_text: Mars has an eccentric orbit (e ≈ 0.0934). Because it moves faster near perihelion, southern summer is short and intense while northern summer is long and mild.
